mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is useful for ensuring the security of your applications by scanning images and running security checks on your clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration to avoid false positives and the potential performance impact during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs Trivy Operator version 0.23.0.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a TrivyConfiguration resource to define how the Trivy Operator should scan images.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aqua.security.trivy/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: my-registry-secret
  severity: HIGH
EOF

```

> Replace 'my-registry-secret' with your actual image pull secret if needed.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aqua.security.trivy/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: my-registry-secret
  severity: HIGH
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify the installation.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related configurations and data.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step upgrades the Trivy Operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfigurations -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-config-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backup existing TrivyConfiguration.
> Upgrades to Trivy Operator version 0.23.0.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyconfigurations -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-config-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivy-operator pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The image pull secret may be missing or incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the image pull secret is correctly configured and exists in the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Trivy scans are not running
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyConfiguration resource may not be set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the TrivyConfiguration with 'kubectl get trivyconfigurations -n trivy-system'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be no resources to scan in the specified namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure that there are workloads in the namespace that need to be scanned.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image scanning policies and enhanced reporting features.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required when upgrading from v0.22.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system'.
codeSnippets:

apiVersion: aqua.security.trivy/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: my-registry-secret
  severity: HIGH
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
